title: NZ Holiday Parks — The Complete Family Guide (TOP 10 vs Freedom Camping)
meta_desc: Discover the best NZ holiday parks, compare TOP 10 vs freedom camping, and get expert booking tips with real prices
intro: When planning a family trip around New Zealand, choosing the right accommodation can be overwhelming. With over 300 holiday parks to choose from, it's essential to know the difference between TOP 10 holiday parks and freedom camping. Here's our comprehensive guide to help you make informed decisions and get the most out of your NZ adventure.

sections.heading: TOP 10 Holiday Parks vs Freedom Camping
sections.body: TOP 10 holiday parks offer a range of facilities, including kitchens, bathrooms, and laundry facilities, whereas freedom camping is a more budget-friendly option with limited amenities. TOP 10 holiday parks typically cost between $40-$60 per night for a powered site, while freedom camping can be as low as $10-$20 per night.

sections.heading: Booking Tips and Tricks
sections.body: Book your holiday park in advance, especially during peak season (December to February). Consider staying at a holiday park that offers a loyalty program, such as TOP 10's membership scheme, which can save you up to 10% on accommodation costs. Be sure to read reviews and check the holiday park's website for any specials or discounts.

sections.heading: Best Holiday Parks by Region
list: North Island: Taupo TOP 10 Holiday Resort, Rotorua TOP 10 Holiday Resort
South Island: Queenstown TOP 10 Holiday Resort, Wanaka Kiwi Holiday Park

sections.heading: Getting Around
sections.body: If you're planning to explore NZ's beautiful landscapes, consider renting a campervan or motorhome. Drive times between major cities can be significant, with Auckland to Rotorua taking around 3 hours, and Queenstown to Wanaka taking around 1 hour. Be sure to check road conditions and plan your itinerary accordingly.

cta.text: Get started with our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator
